name: Chana Masala
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight and pressure cook until soft (or use canned).
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli. Cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add boiled chickpeas with some water. Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon. Serve with rice or naan.

name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, then transfer to ice water.
Blend blanched spinach into a smooth puree.
Heat butter and oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add finely chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook for 1 minute.
Add spinach puree and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add cubed paneer and garam masala. Cook for 5 more minutes.
Add a splash of cream, mix well. Serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Dal Tadka
instructions: Wash and pressure cook yellow lentils with turmeric until soft.
In a separate pan, heat ghee. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped garlic, ginger, green chilli, and onion. Sauté until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add red chilli powder and coriander powder. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour the tadka over the cooked dal and mix well.
Simmer for 5-10 minutes. Garnish with coriander and lemon juice.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Aloo Gobi
instructions: Cut potatoes and cauliflower into bite-sized florets.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until translucent.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add potatoes and cauliflower. Stir to coat with spices.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with roti.

name: Vegetable Biryani
instructions: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Cook until 70% done with whole spices and salt.
Heat ghee in a pan. Add sliced onions and fry until golden brown. Set aside.
In the same pan, add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli. Sauté for 1 minute.
Add mixed vegetables and all dry spices. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add yogurt and cook until vegetables are tender.
Layer the cooked rice over the vegetable mixture.
Sprinkle fried onions, saffron soaked in warm milk, coriander, and mint.
Cover tightly and cook on dum (very low heat) for 15-20 minutes.
Gently mix before serving. Serve with raita.

name: Masoor Dal (Red Lentil Curry)
instructions: Wash red lentils thoroughly. Cook with turmeric and water until soft.
Heat ghee in a small pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped garlic, ginger, green chilli, and onion. Sauté until golden.
Add chopped tomato and cook until soft.
Add coriander powder and red chilli powder. Mix well.
Pour this tadka over the cooked dal. Stir and simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and lemon juice. Serve with rice.

name: Baingan Bharta (Smoky Eggplant Mash)
instructions: Roast the eggplant directly on a gas flame or in oven until skin is charred and flesh is soft.
Let it cool, then peel off the skin and mash the flesh.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates.
Add all spice powders and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add peas and mashed eggplant. Mix well and cook for 10 minutes.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with roti or paratha.

name: Aloo Matar (Potato Pea Curry)
instructions: Boil potatoes until just tender. Peel and cube them.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add peas and boiled potatoes. Mix well.
Add water for gravy and simmer for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander. Serve with roti or rice.

name: Mushroom Matar (Mushroom Pea Curry)
instructions: Slice mushrooms. Heat oil in a pan.
Add cumin seeds, then chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Sauté until onions are golden. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add mushrooms and peas. Cook for 8-10 minutes.
Add a splash of cream and garam masala. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with naan or rice.

name: Paneer Butter Masala
instructions: Blanch tomatoes, then blend with cashews into a smooth puree.
Heat butter in a pan. Add finely chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Sauté until onions are golden brown.
Add the tomato-cashew puree. Cook until oil separates and mixture thickens.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add cubed paneer and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add cream, garam masala, and a pinch of sugar. Mix well.
Garnish with coriander. Serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Vegetable Pulao
instructions: Wash and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes.
Heat ghee in a pot. Add whole spices (cumin, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaves).
Add sliced onion, garlic, and ginger. Sauté until onions are translucent.
Add mixed vegetables and turmeric. Sauté for 2 minutes.
Add soaked rice and salt. Stir gently.
Add water (1:1.5 ratio of rice to water). Bring to a boil.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes.
Let it rest for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork and garnish with coriander.

name: Gobi Manchurian
instructions: Cut cauliflower into florets and blanch in salted water for 3 minutes. Drain well.
Make a batter with flour, corn flour, salt, pepper, and water. Dip florets in batter.
Deep fry the battered florets until golden and crisp. Set aside.
Heat oil in a wok. Add chopped garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Add finely chopped onions and capsicum. Stir fry on high heat for 2 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, and a splash of water. Mix well.
Add fried cauliflower and toss to coat evenly.
Garnish with spring onions/coriander. Serve hot.

name: Dal Makhani (Creamy Black Lentils)
instructions: Soak black lentils and kidney beans overnight. Pressure cook until very soft.
Heat butter in a pan. Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the cooked dal and beans. Mix well and simmer on low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add cream and garam masala. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander and a pat of butter. Serve with naan.

name: Bhindi Masala (Okra Stir Fry)
instructions: Wash and dry okra thoroughly. Slice into 1-inch pieces.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until onions are translucent.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt.
Add okra and mix well. Cook on medium heat without covering for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala and cook for 2 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander and lemon juice. Serve with roti.

name: Cabbage Thoran (Stir Fried Cabbage with Coconut)
instructions: Finely shred cabbage. Grate fresh coconut.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them pop.
Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, and green chilli.
Add shredded cabbage, turmeric, and salt. Mix well.
Cook on medium heat, uncovered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add grated coconut and mix. Cook for 2 more minutes.
Serve as a side dish with rice and dal.

name: Chickpea Spinach Curry
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add chopped spinach and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes.
Add chickpeas (canned or boiled). Mix well.
Add water for desired consistency and simmer for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle garam masala and lemon juice. Serve with rice.

name: Mediterranean Buddha Bowl
instructions: Cook quinoa according to package directions.
Toss chickpeas with olive oil, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast at 200°C for 15 minutes.
Steam or roast broccoli until tender.
Slice bell pepper, cucumber, tomato, and avocado.
Make dressing: mix tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and water until smooth.
Assemble bowl: quinoa at base, topped with chickpeas, broccoli, and fresh veggies.
Drizzle with tahini dressing and enjoy.

name: Pasta Primavera
instructions: Cook pasta according to package instructions. Reserve some pasta water.
Heat olive oil in a large pan. Add minced garlic and red chilli flakes.
Add chopped broccoli, zucchini, and bell pepper. Sauté for 5 minutes.
Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add cooked pasta to the vegetables. Toss well, adding pasta water as needed.
Season with salt, pepper, fresh basil, and parsley.
Squeeze lemon juice on top. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Mushroom Stir Fry
instructions: Slice mushrooms, capsicum, and onion.
Heat oil in a wok or pan on high heat.
Add minced garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add onions and capsicum. Stir fry for 2 minutes.
Add mushrooms and stir fry for 5 minutes until golden.
Add soy sauce, pepper, and salt. Toss well.
Garnish with coriander and lemon juice. Serve as a side or with rice.

name: Lentil Soup (Spanish-style)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add chopped onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add diced carrots and potato. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add chopped tomato, paprika, cumin, bay leaves, salt, and pepper.
Add washed lentils and 1 liter of water or vegetable broth.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 25-30 minutes until lentils are soft.
Adjust seasoning. Squeeze in lemon juice.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley.

name: Ratatouille
instructions: Dice all vegetables into similar-sized pieces.
Heat olive oil in a large pan. Add chopped onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add eggplant and cook for 5 minutes.
Add zucchini and bell pepper. Cook for 5 more minutes.
Add tomatoes, thyme, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 25 minutes.
Stir in fresh basil and parsley.
Serve hot as a main dish with bread or as a side.

name: Upma (South Indian Savory Semolina)
instructions: Dry roast semolina in a pan until lightly golden. Set aside.
Heat oil in the same pan. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli, and ginger.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add peanuts and sauté for 1 minute.
Add turmeric, salt, sugar, and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil.
Slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Cover and cook on low heat for 5 minutes.
Garnish with lemon juice, coriander, and grated coconut. Serve hot.

name: Pongal (South Indian Rice & Lentil Porridge)
instructions: Wash rice and moong dal. Roast moong dal until fragrant.
Cook rice and dal together with water and salt until very soft and mushy.
Heat ghee in a small pan. Add cumin seeds, black pepper, ginger, curry leaves, and cashews.
Fry until cashews are golden and pour this tadka over the pongal.
Mix well. Serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Aloo Paratha (Stuffed Potato Flatbread)
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Add chopped green chilli, ginger, and spices.
Add fresh coriander and lemon juice. Mix well.
Knead dough with flour, salt, and water. Rest for 15 minutes.
Divide dough into balls. Roll each into a small circle.
Place potato filling in center, close edges, and roll out gently into a flatbread.
Cook on a hot griddle with butter until golden brown on both sides.
Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Veg Korma (Mixed Vegetables in Creamy Gravy)
instructions: Blend together a paste of cashew, coconut, yogurt, and a little water.
Heat ghee in a pan. Add whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon).
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until golden.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add the cashew-coconut paste. Mix well.
Add coriander powder, turmeric, salt, and water. Simmer until vegetables are cooked.
Add cream and garam masala. Cook for 2 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or naan.

name: Sambhar (South Indian Lentil & Vegetable Stew)
instructions: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft. Mash well.
Cook mixed vegetables (pumpkin, carrot, okra, etc.) in water until tender.
Extract tamarind juice from tamarind paste.
Add tamarind juice, sambar powder, salt, and cooked dal to the vegetables. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Heat oil in a small pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves, and red chilli.
Pour this tadka over the sambar. Mix well.
Simmer for 5 more minutes. Garnish with coriander.
Serve hot with rice or idli.

name: Galician Vegetable Stew (Caldo de Verduras)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add chopped onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add chopped carrots, green beans, and cabbage. Sauté for 5 minutes.
Add diced potatoes, bay leaves, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Cover with water or vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Simmer for 25-30 minutes until all vegetables are tender.
Adjust seasoning. Remove bay leaves.
Serve hot with crusty Galician bread.

name: Vegetable Pasta Bake
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil. Sauté garlic, then add all chopped vegetables.
Cook for 5-7 minutes. Add herbs, salt, and pepper.
Mix vegetables with pasta. Transfer to a baking dish.
Top with grated cheese. Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes until golden.
Serve hot with bread.

name: Lemon Rice
instructions: Cook rice and let it cool slightly.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli, and ginger.
Add peanuts and sauté until golden.
Add turmeric powder and salt. Mix well.
Add the cooked rice and mix gently.
Squeeze lemon juice over the rice and mix well.
Garnish with coriander and grated coconut. Serve at room temperature.

name: Tofu Tikka Masala
instructions: Cube tofu. Marinate with yogurt, tandoori masala, turmeric, and salt for 20 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry the tofu cubes until golden. Set aside.
Heat butter in a pan. Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Sauté until golden. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add cumin, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add a splash of cream and water for gravy. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add grilled tofu and simmer for 5 more minutes.
Garnish with coriander and lemon juice. Serve with naan or rice.

name: Curd Rice (South Indian Yogurt Rice)
instructions: Cook rice and let it cool to room temperature.
Mash the rice lightly with a spoon or hands.
Add yogurt and milk. Mix well until creamy. Add salt.
Heat oil in a small pan. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli, and ginger.
Pour this tadka over the curd rice. Mix well.
Garnish with coriander and pomegranate seeds.
Serve at room temperature. Perfect for a light meal.

name: Vegetable Stir Fry Noodles
instructions: Cook noodles according to package. Drain and toss with a little oil.
Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
Add minced garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add sliced carrots, cabbage, capsicum, and mushrooms. Stir fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Toss well.
Add cooked noodles and toss everything together for 2 minutes.
Garnish with coriander. Serve hot.

name: Pumpkin Coconut Curry
instructions: Peel and cube the pumpkin into bite-size pieces.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli, and ginger.
Add chopped onion and garlic. Sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder. Cook for 1 minute.
Add pumpkin cubes and salt. Stir to coat with spices.
Add coconut milk and water. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes until pumpkin is tender.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice.

name: Tortilla de Patatas (Spanish Omelette)
instructions: Peel and thinly slice potatoes and onion.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan. Add potatoes and onion.
Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender (about 15 minutes).
Beat eggs in a bowl with salt and pepper.
Drain the potatoes/onion and add to the beaten eggs. Mix well.
Pour the mixture back into the pan with a little oil.
Cook on low heat for 5 minutes, then flip and cook the other side for 3 minutes.
Serve hot or at room temperature.

name: Chickpea & Spinach Tortilla Bowl
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan. Add chopped onion and garlic. Sauté until soft.
Add cumin and paprika. Cook for 30 seconds.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 3 minutes.
Add chickpeas and spinach. Cook until spinach wilts.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
Serve in a bowl with crusty bread or tortilla.

name: Mushroom Masala
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Okra should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add ginger and curry leaves and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add okra, chickpeas, and cauliflower and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 13 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Paneer Curry
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Peas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add coriander powder, turmeric, garam masala and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add peas, masoor dal, and pumpkin and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 24 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Bhindi Subji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Okra should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add okra, black lentils, and mixed vegetables and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Chana Tadka
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add coriander powder, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add eggplant, black lentils, and urad dal and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 16 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Mushroom Curry
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, coriander powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add eggplant, mushroom, and moong dal and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Baingan Bhurji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add eggplant, green beans, and carrot and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 21 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Mushroom Tadka
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.

Add chickpeas, toor dal, and black lentils and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 23 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Mushroom Subji

Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 17 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Paneer Tadka
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add ginger and curry leaves and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, garam masala and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add eggplant, paneer, and red lentils and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 19 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Aloo Curry
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Peas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, garam masala and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add peas, black lentils, and mixed vegetables and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Chana Sabzi
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chickpeas, eggplant, and broccoli and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 22 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Dal Bhurji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Bell pepper should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add bell pepper, black lentils, and green beans and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Chana Fry
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Black lentils should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, garam masala, mustard seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add black lentils, cauliflower, and paneer and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 18 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Palak Tadka
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.

Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Baingan Masala
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Black lentils should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.

Add black lentils, paneer, and tofu and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 21 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Mixed vegetables should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add coriander powder, turmeric, garam masala and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add mixed vegetables, cauliflower, and sweet potato and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 19 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.

Add chickpeas, toor dal, and red lentils and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 16 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 14 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Paneer Bhurji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Peas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add peas, black lentils, and paneer and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Bhindi Sabzi
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and curry leaves and sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.

Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Bhindi Masala
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add coriander powder, mustard seeds, cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chickpeas, peas, and bell pepper and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 13 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Matar Sabzi
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Peas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, mustard seeds and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add peas, toor dal, and red lentils and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 22 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Chana Bhurji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Peas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, turmeric, coriander powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add peas, toor dal, and pumpkin and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 24 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Matar Subji
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Eggplant should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and coriander and sauté until golden.
Add turmeric, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add eggplant, mushroom, and masoor dal and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 21 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Matar Tadka
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Chickpeas should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large pan. Add onion and ginger and sauté until golden.
Add cumin, garam masala, red chilli powder and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chickpeas, urad dal, and green beans and mix well with the spices.
Season with salt, cover and cook on low heat for 23 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Dal Fry
instructions: Wash and prepare all ingredients. Okra should be chopped into bite-sized pieces.

name: Garlic Tofu Style
instructions: Heat oil in a wok over high heat.
Add minced ginger and garlic. Stir fry for 30 seconds.
Add garlic and peas and soy sauce and stir fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce and toss well.
Cook for another 2 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender.
Serve hot with steamed rice or noodles.

name: Spinach Curry
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan. Add garlic and ginger.

Add curry powder and salt. Mix well.
Cook for another 10 minutes until everything is cooked through.
Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Eggplant Gratin
instructions: Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot.
Add garlic and onions. Cook until softened.
Add eggplant and garlic and bell pepper and stir well.
Add herbs, salt, and pepper. Cover with water.
Simmer gently for 25-30 minutes until tender and flavours meld.
Serve hot with crusty bread.

name: Green Beans Kootu Curry
instructions: Wash and chop green beans finely.

Serve as a side dish with rice.

name: Chilli Mushroom
instructions: Prepare a batter with flour, corn flour, salt, and water.
Dip vegetables in batter and deep fry until golden.
Heat oil in a wok. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and a little water. Bring to a simmer.
Add fried vegetables and toss to coat evenly.
Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

Heat oil in a wok. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilli.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, and a little water. Bring to a simmer.
Add fried vegetables and toss to coat evenly.
Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: Sweet Potato Soup With Coconut
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add ginger and garlic and sauté.
Add curry powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add sweet potato and pumpkin and broccoli and sauté for 3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and garnish with coriander. Serve with rice.
